title: Ericoides hispidulum
taxonomy: Ericoides hispidulum is a species of flowering plant, and its classification is as follows: it belongs to the family Iridaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as irises. This plant is further classified as being within the genus Ericoides, which is a small group of plants often confused with the genus Eric in the heath family. The specific classification of Ericoides hispidulum is a bit more complex, and it is often placed in the subgenus which is sometimes referred to as the "South African" or "African" group within the Iridaceae family. The classification is mainly based on its physical and morphological characteristics, such as its leaf shape, flower color, and growth habits. By classifying Ericoides hispidulum in this way, botanists can better understand its evolutionary history and relationships with other plants.
